subject: Public Health
title: Open Defecation and Its Dangers
description: A 60-second lesson on Open Defecation and Its Dangers.

hook.text: 494 million people worldwide still defecate in the open—contaminating water, soil, and food.

buildup.text: Open defecation spreads cholera, typhoid, hepatitis, and intestinal parasites through the fecal-oral route.

discovery.text: Children in communities with open defecation are shorter and sicker—chronic fecal exposure stunts growth.

twist.text: India's Swachh Bharat mission built 110 million toilets in 5 years—the largest sanitation campaign ever.

climax.text: Building toilets isn't enough—behavior change and maintenance are essential for lasting improvement.

punchline.text: A toilet is public health infrastructure disguised as a simple seat.

quiz.question: How many toilets did India's Swachh Bharat mission build?
